message:
Port dns_lookup to the neo executor (#6034)

## 🔍 Problem

- `dns_lookup` still only works on the legacy executor.
- Neo pipelines cannot use the operator yet.
- The migrated neo path also needs deterministic tests and correct cache
semantics.

## 🛠️ Solution

- Add a neo `dns_lookup` implementation backed by the async DNS
resolvers.
- Keep the legacy operator path in place for compatibility.
- Report resolver startup failures, age cached forward TTLs, and switch
the migrated neo tests to loopback-based cases.

## 💬 Review

- Focus on the new executor implementation in
`libtenzir/builtins/operators/dns_lookup.cpp`.
- Check the async resolver cache handling in
`libtenzir/src/async/dns.cpp`.
- The test migration intentionally avoids public DNS for the updated neo
cases.

## 📖 Documentation PR

- https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/262

<sub>resolves TNZ-84</sub>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +0,0 @@
-from {
-  // Cloudflare DNS - stable IP
-  ip: 1.1.1.1,
-  name: "test"
-}
-dns_lookup ip

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+from {host: "127.0.0.1", addr: ip("127.0.0.1")},
+     {host: "127.0.0.1", addr: ip("127.0.0.1")}
+dns_lookup host
+dns_lookup addr, result=reverse

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+{
+  host: "127.0.0.1",
+  addr: 127.0.0.1,
+  dns_lookup: [
+    {
+      address: 127.0.0.1,
+      type: "A",
+      ttl: 1min,
+    },
+  ],
+  reverse: {
+    hostname: "localhost",
+  },
+}
+{
+  host: "127.0.0.1",
+  addr: 127.0.0.1,
+  dns_lookup: [
+    {
+      address: 127.0.0.1,
+      type: "A",
+      ttl: 1min,
+    },
+  ],
+  reverse: {
+    hostname: "localhost",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+from {
+  // Loopback IP resolved without network access
+  ip: 127.0.0.1,
+  name: "test"
+}
+dns_lookup ip

@@ -6,4 +6,4 @@ dns_lookup host
 unroll dns_lookup
 drop dns_lookup.ttl
 where dns_lookup.type == "A"
-sort dns_lookup.type, dns_lookup.address
+sort dns_lookup.address

@@ -6,4 +6,4 @@ dns_lookup host
 unroll dns_lookup
 where dns_lookup.type == "A"
 drop dns_lookup.ttl
-sort dns_lookup.type, dns_lookup.address
+sort dns_lookup.address
